White House Conference for a Drug Free America, subtitle S (§ 1931 et seq.), , , as amended by , , , established the White House Conference for a Drug Free America, provided for its purpose, responsibilities, participants, authorization of appropriations, and required a final report to be submitted not later than , with the President to report annually to Congress for three years thereafter on the status and implementation of the findings and recommendations of the Conference.Pub.
